race.name: Vasaloppet USA
race.tagline: The Swedish legend transplanted to the frozen lakes and birch forests of Minnesota — fifty-eight kilometers of Nordic heartland grit.

vitals.registration: Online registration, typically opens in fall
vitals.aid_stations: Multiple feed stations with warm drinks and energy food

climate.description: Minnesota February delivers reliably cold conditions. Temperatures typically range from -20°C to -5°C. Wind chill across open lake sections can be severe. Snow cover is generally dependable but warm spells can compromise trail quality.
challenges: Extreme wind chill across open terrain
Wax selection for wide temperature swings during the day
Occasional warm spells degrade snow quality

course.primary: Point-to-point through Minnesota lake country, rolling terrain on predominantly flat-to-gently-undulating trails. The course winds through birch and pine forest with sections crossing frozen lakes.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through forest and lake terrain
course.grooming: Well-groomed with classic tracks and skate lane
features: Named after the original Swedish Vasaloppet
Finishes in Mora, Minnesota — named after Mora, Sweden
Course through birch and pine forests reminiscent of Dalarna

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Strong community and field size anchored by Scandinavian heritage. Flat terrain and modest scenery limit ceiling. Solid organization and accessible location for Midwest skiers.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 eligible for 1-tier promotion, already at T2

history.summary: Founded in 1973 by a group of Scandinavian-Americans in Mora, Minnesota — a town named after the Swedish finish city of the original Vasaloppet. The race was created to honor Nordic ski heritage in the Upper Midwest, where generations of Swedish and Norwegian immigrants settled. It has grown into the largest cross-country ski race in the United States, attracting around 3,000 participants across all distances.
notable_facts: Mora, Minnesota was founded by Swedish immigrants and named after the Vasaloppet finish town
Largest cross-country ski race in the United States
Licensed by the Swedish Vasaloppet organization
Celebrates the deep Scandinavian-American heritage of Minnesota
Originally 55km, adjusted over the years to current 58km course

1:30pm - The Miniloppet Children's Races (500m, 1km, 4km) - Starts Library Park

videos.title: 2011 Mora Vasaloppet - 35K Freestyle Recap
videos.description: One of the biggest fields in event history clashed with some of the warmest conditions providing some challenging racing for the 2011 Mora Vasaloppet 35K freestyle race.  Nathan Porath broke away from the lead pack after the infamous big hill and never looked back, repeating as mens champion.  Angie Robinson reeled in the womens leaders then pressed the pace, moving out front to collect the womens title.

videos.title: Introducing Moraloppet Ski for the Cure Ribbon Tying
videos.description: The ribbon tying event announcing the new purpose of the Moraloppet cross country ski race - the Moraloppet Ski for the Cure.
videos.transcript:
okay well welcome uh today
we're kind of excited to formally
announce a major change
in our moral opinion which uh has been a
very traditional race that's been held
the week before bustle open weekend
and so today it's a pretty major change
and from today forward
uh the moral open is going to be known
as the moral open ski for the cure
so we're pretty proud of that and also
with our new partnership
and association with the susan g komen
organization and we have karen here who
shall talk in a little bit we
we did practice this last night it
worked wonderfully
there you go
um we have three honored guests like
wade said
and um they have of course
something in common first off something
very positive they are cranscoulas
and the second item is of course
the three of them have had breast cancer
this is joanne olin
hi um i've got a couple things to say
i was diagnosed with breast cancer back
in 2007
i went through six months of treatment
which took me to the end of june 2008
so next month i am calling myself a
three-year survivor
hurry
okay next is marge booganoogan
yeah i was diagnosed last october
and i had a mammogram a year ago
it was clear and the next time i had the
mammogram it wasn't
so um i had surgery on the 15th of
november
then i had radiation and i had
six chemotherapies
